seoTitle: How to Use Canva Whiteboard for Team Brainstorming
metaDescription: Get started with Canva Whiteboard for brainstorming, mind maps, and team collaboration, including tips for running a productive session remotely.
intro: Canva Whiteboard gives teams a shared visual space to brainstorm, plan, and collaborate in real time, which is especially useful for remote workshops and classroom sessions. This guide walks through setting up a whiteboard, using its core tools, and applying it to real planning scenarios.

steps.heading: Open and set up your Whiteboard
steps.body: Create a new Whiteboard from Canva's design options, giving you a blank collaborative canvas to start adding ideas to.

steps.heading: Explore the core tools
steps.body: Add sticky notes, shapes, text, and images to lay out ideas visually, using the same drag-and-drop approach as standard Canva designs.

steps.heading: Invite others to collaborate
steps.body: Share the Whiteboard link with teammates so they can contribute in real time during a workshop, class, or planning session.

steps.heading: Organize ideas into mind maps or flowcharts
steps.body: Arrange contributed notes and shapes into structured layouts like mind maps or flowcharts to turn scattered ideas into a clear plan.

steps.heading: Apply it to a real session
steps.body: Use the Whiteboard live during a meeting, class, or workshop to capture input as it happens rather than compiling notes afterward.

faq.question: Can multiple people edit a Canva Whiteboard at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, Whiteboard is built for real-time collaboration, so invited teammates can add and move elements simultaneously.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Canva plan to use Whiteboard?
faq.answer: Canva Whiteboard is accessible on standard Canva plans, making it approachable for individuals and small teams to try out.

faq.question: Can I turn a Whiteboard session into a presentation afterward?
faq.answer: Since Whiteboard uses the same design elements as other Canva tools, you can repurpose the content into presentations or documents later.

faq.question: Is Canva Whiteboard good for remote teams?
faq.answer: Yes, it's particularly useful for remote workshops and online classes where a shared visual space helps replace an in-person whiteboard.
